title: Add requirements sync workflow and fix semantic-release package versioning

comments.author: derek.norrbom
comments.text: Implemented: (1) added .github/workflows/requirements-sync.yml to pip-compile requirements.txt from pyproject.toml on non-main pushes and auto-commit via ANTHUSBOT_GH_TOKEN; (2) fixed semantic-release config in pyproject.toml by switching from invalid `version_variable` to `version_variables` and using `version_source = "file"`; (3) aligned prerelease branch config to `dev`; (4) removed CI bootstrap step that force-tagged v0.0.0 and updated release branch condition to dev. Validation: TOML/YAML parse OK and isolated semantic-release simulation on main produced release commit updating plexus/_version.py to 1.20.4.

comments.author: derek.norrbom
comments.text: Added release-safety guard in .github/workflows/ci.yaml before semantic-release: computes latest released version and computed next version from semantic-release CLI, and fails if next < latest. This prevents accidental downgrade-style releases (e.g., 1.20.3 after published v1.21.0) and protects existing GitHub releases while release history/config is reconciled.

comments.author: derek.norrbom
comments.text: Recommended best-practice path: keep semantic-release safety gate active to protect existing GitHub releases; treat requirements sync change as independent. For release lineage reconciliation, do a one-time canonical release point on main (release-style commit + tag) at > v1.21.0 (e.g., v1.21.1), then keep all future tags/release commits produced only by semantic-release automation (no manual release tags on merge commits). This avoids branch-history drift where latest tag exists but algorithm baseline remains older.

comments.author: derek.norrbom
comments.text: Investigating PR #105 blocked by expected CI checks not reporting. Collecting PR check/run metadata and workflow trigger diagnostics to identify why CI jobs are pending with no status.

comments.author: derek.norrbom
comments.text: Diagnosed PR #105 deadlock: semantic-release on dev push created a new bot commit (03aa60f) after CI, and because push used GITHUB_TOKEN, GitHub did not trigger a fresh CI suite for the new SHA. Updated ci.yaml so release safety check + semantic-release run only on push to main. This prevents bot-mutated dev head commits from leaving required PR checks permanently pending.

comments.author: derek.norrbom
comments.text: Investigating requirements-sync run 22978450860 blocked by ruleset despite release-bot bypass. Collecting run logs + ruleset actor/bypass details to identify mismatch.

comments.author: derek.norrbom
comments.text: Root cause confirmed for run 22978450860: failure is from CI semantic-release push on main using GH_TOKEN=secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN (github-actions bot), not from requirements-sync. Patched ci.yaml so semantic-release push config uses ANTHUSBOT_GH_TOKEN, sets bot git identity, rewrites origin remote with bot token, and uses GH_TOKEN/GITHUB_TOKEN from ANTHUSBOT_GH_TOKEN only for release step.

comments.author: derek.norrbom
comments.text: Updating requirements generation script to prepend explanatory comments in requirements.txt (auto-generated, do not edit manually, intended for Snyk dependency scanning).

comments.author: derek.norrbom
comments.text: Investigated Snyk requirements scan failure. Tested lambda-style VCS dependency syntax (git+ URL without package-name prefix) and confirmed pip install behavior is equivalent. Local Snyk still fails with internal resolver traceback: FileNotFoundError for packaging-25.0.dist-info/METADATA. Also reproduced that clean Linux env without git fails any VCS dependency install. Next step: normalize generated requirements VCS lines to lambda-style and add guard comments/recommendation for Snyk-compatible manifest.

comments.author: derek.norrbom
comments.text: Validated why simplified requirements still fail locally. Root cause was local miniconda corruption: packaging-25.0.dist-info missing METADATA, which caused Snyk internal resolver crash. After repairing packaging, Snyk error changed to expected 'Required packages missing: dateparser' (local mode requires deps installed) and passes with --skip-unresolved.

comments.author: derek.norrbom
comments.text: Root-cause validation for PR #109 Snyk check: in a clean Python resolver environment (python:3.11-slim), installing generated root requirements fails immediately on VCS dependency line because git binary is absent (Cannot find command 'git'). This matches 'Packages installation failed' behavior from Snyk Git integration. Lambda manifest may appear healthy because unchanged manifests are not necessarily part of the PR check scope.
